name: Route of the Three Manors
name_original: Negreira – sendero Fervenza de Pozo Negro
description: Hike the 9.6-mile Negreira – Fervenza de Pozo Negro trail to discover a magical waterfall and lush oak groves in Galicia.
region_intro: Hiking around Negreira offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by lush oak groves, verdant forests, and the prominent Tambre River. The town is nestled in a valley surrounded by rolling hills and mountains, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. This region is a significant stop on the Camino de Fisterra, featuring well-established trail networks that integrate natural beauty with historical landmarks. The topography includes river valleys, forested hills, and moderate elevation changes. Best hiking trails in Negreira The most popular hiking route is Route of the Three Manors, a 9.6 miles (15.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 13 minutes to complete. This route follows the Xacobean way, blending natural scenery with architectural heritage, including historic manor houses. Another top favourite among local hikers is Augapesada Bridge – Augapesada Bridge loop from A Ponte Maceira, a difficult 11.8 miles (18.9 km) path. This trail features scenic forest paths and varying elevation gains, typical of the Camino de Fisterra. Local hikers also love the Casa Pita – Santa Mariña loop from Vilaserío, a 14.3 miles (23.0 km) trail leading through forested areas and rural landscapes, often completed in about 6 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Negreira is defined by lush oak groves, river valleys, and historical landmarks like medieval bridges and chapels. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Negreira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from around 80 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Negreira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Negreira?
A: Negreira offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by lush oak groves, verdant forests, and the prominent Tambre River. The town is nestled in a valley surrounded by rolling hills and mountains, providing varied terrain from river valleys to forested hills with moderate elevation changes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Negreira?
A: Yes, Negreira has several easy trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6971420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of San Mauro – Camino de Fisterra loop from Negreira&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.6 km hike that takes about 2 hours to complete, perfect for a relaxed outing.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural attractions can I discover on the trails?
A: Hiking trails around Negreira often integrate historical and natural landmarks. You can encounter the beautiful medieval Roman bridge &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/695897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Ponte Maceira&lt;/a&gt;, which crosses the Tambre River and features a waterfall. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/654411" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eucalyptus Forest on the Camino de Fisterra&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Negreira town&lt;/a&gt; itself.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Negreira?
A: Yes, many routes in Negreira are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31867722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Fisterra – Scenic Forest Path loop from Negreira&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.1 km trail.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Negreira?
A: The region's lush landscapes suggest that spring and autumn would be ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially in the shaded oak groves and forests, but it's advisable to carry enough water.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Negreira dog-friendly?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails can vary, the natural and rural character of Negreira's trails, including forest paths and riverside walks, generally makes them suitable for dogs. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and clean up after them.

Q: How long do hikes in Negreira typically take?
A: Hikes in Negreira vary in length and duration. You can find shorter walks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6971420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of San Mauro – Camino de Fisterra loop&lt;/a&gt; which takes about 2 hours, to longer, more challenging routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38146737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Pita – Santa Mariña loop from Vilaserío&lt;/a&gt;, which can take over 6 hours.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Negreira?
A: The hiking routes in Negreira are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from around 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the quiet woodlands, well-marked paths, and the blend of natural beauty with historical elements.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in the area?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38290664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Augapesada Bridge – Augapesada Bridge loop from A Ponte Maceira&lt;/a&gt; offer a difficult 18.9 km path with significant elevation gains, typical of the Camino de Fisterra stages.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Negreira?
A: Given Negreira's role as a stop on the Camino de Fisterra and its rural setting, parking is generally available in or near the town and at various starting points for trails. It's advisable to check specific trailhead information for the best parking options.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking routes?
A: Negreira, being a significant stop on the Camino de Fisterra, has infrastructure for walkers. You can find various accommodations, including hostels like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/418318" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostel, Santa Marina&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6144682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albergue Alto da Pena&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5512556" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albergue O Refuxio da Ponte&lt;/a&gt;, as well as cafes and restaurants in the town and surrounding villages.

Q: Do I need any special permits to hike in Negreira?
A: Based on available information, there are no specific permits required for general hiking on the public trails around Negreira. The trails are well-established and accessible for outdoor enthusiasts.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Negreira – Fervenza de Pozo Negro trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as moderate. While the full 15.4 km route with 250 meters of elevation gain requires a good level of fitness, the paths are generally accessible. Be aware of steep descents and potentially slippery terrain when approaching the Pozo Negro Waterfall.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: You'll traverse varied terrain, including beautiful oak groves and native woodlands that provide shade. Near the waterfall, expect steep descents. The path may also follow the banks of the Tambre River, characterized by lush vegetation. Some sections might be slippery, especially close to the waterfall.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: The main highlight is the stunning Pozo Negro Waterfall, formed by the Chavielos River merging with the Tambre. You'll also pass through the historic town of Negreira, where you can see the Pilgrim Monument. Other notable features include ethnographic elements like "hórreos" (raised granaries), wash houses, and fountains in villages like Ons de Abaixo. If you extend your route, you might encounter the Torre de Altamira and "pesqueiras" (ancient fish traps) along the river.

faqs.question: Is the Negreira – Fervenza de Pozo Negro trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is under control, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and be mindful of other hikers. Remember to pack out any waste.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer provides shade from the oak groves, but can be warmer. Winter hikes are possible, but some sections near the waterfall might be more slippery due to moisture.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Negreira – Fervenza de Pozo Negro trail or to visit the waterfall.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Negreira – Fervenza de Pozo Negro trail?
faqs.answer: Parking is typically available in Negreira, which serves as a common starting point for the trail. Look for designated parking areas within the town. Specific trailhead parking for the waterfall itself might be limited, so starting from Negreira is often recommended.

faqs.question: Can I reach the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: Yes, Negreira is accessible by public transport, making it a convenient starting point for the trail. Check local bus schedules for routes connecting to Negreira from nearby towns or cities.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities near the trail?
faqs.answer: The town of Negreira offers various amenities, including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options, perfect for before or after your hike. Along the trail itself, facilities are limited, so plan accordingly.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other official trails, including the Camiño de Fisterra-Muxía, Mtb Fisterra-Muxía, and Ruta dos Tres Pazos. You may also encounter parts of the Camiño do Monte Pequeno and Camiño de Fisterra.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: While the full 15.4 km route might be long for younger children, shorter, less difficult sections directly to the Pozo Negro Waterfall are often considered enjoyable walks for families. Be mindful of the steep and potentially slippery sections near the waterfall.
